Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a participating team in the cryptic hunt, tracking their progress and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Team entity.

teamName.description: The unique name chosen by the team for identification.

currentLevelId.description: Reference to the current Level entity the team is attempting or has solved. (Relationship: Team 1:N Level)

flagCount.description: The cumulative count of times the team has been flagged for suspicious activity.

penaltyUntil.description: Timestamp indicating the exact date and time until which a penalty is active for the team. If null or past, no penalty is active.

Level.title: Level
Level.description: Defines a single cryptic hunt level, including its question and order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Level entity.

question.description: The text content of the cryptic question for this level.

order.description: The sequential order of this level within the overall hunt.

Attempt.title: Attempt
Attempt.description: Records each submission attempt made by a team for a particular level, including outcome and metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attempt entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team entity that made this attempt. (Relationship: Team 1:N Attempt)

levelId.description: Reference to the Level entity for which this attempt was made. (Relationship: Level 1:N Attempt)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the attempt was recorded.

isCorrect.description: A boolean indicating whether the submitted answer was correct for the level.

ip.description: The IP address from which the attempt originated, used for security and monitoring.

Flag.title: Flag
Flag.description: Documents instances where a team has been identified for suspicious or irregular activity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flag entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team entity that was flagged. (Relationship: Team 1:N Flag)

reason.description: A descriptive explanation of why the team was flagged.

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the flag was recorded.

Hint.title: Hint
Hint.description: Stores a hint associated with a specific level, including its content and release status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hint entity.

levelId.description: Reference to the Level entity to which this hint belongs. (Relationship: Level 1:N Hint)

hintText.description: The textual content of the hint.

isReleased.description: A boolean indicating whether the hint has been officially released and is visible to teams.

releasedAt.description: The exact date and time when the hint was released.

AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an administrative user account with elevated privileges to manage the hunt. This entity is assumed for referential integrity with AdminLog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity.

username.description: The unique username used by the administrator for login.

AdminLog.title: AdminLog
AdminLog.description: Records every administrative action performed within the application for auditing and monitoring purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminLog entity.

adminId.description: Reference to the AdminUser entity who performed this action. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N AdminLog)

action.description: A descriptive string indicating the type of action performed (e.g., 'releaseHint', 'applyPenalty').

metadata.description: A JSON string containing additional, structured data relevant to the specific action (e.g., {'teamId': 'xyz', 'penaltyDuration': 45}).

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the administrative action was performed.

definition.description: Represents a participating team. Documents are owned by the team, where `teamId` matches `request.auth.uid` for authorization.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Defines a single cryptic hunt level, containing public information (question, order). Sensitive data like answer hashes are present but restricted by security rules for client reads.

params.name: levelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the level.

definition.description: Records each submission attempt made by a team for a level. Owned by the team, where `teamId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'teamId' from path for authorization independence.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team that made the attempt, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attempt.

definition.description: Documents instances of suspicious activity. Only accessible by administrators through Cloud Functions for writes and direct read for admin dashboard.

params.name: flagId
params.description: The unique identifier for the flag.

definition.description: Stores hints associated with a level. Access is restricted based on the `isReleased` field and the team's `currentLevelId`. Includes implicit `levelId` from the path for authorization context.

params.name: levelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the level to which the hint belongs.

params.name: hintId
params.description: The unique identifier for the hint.

definition.description: Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant administrative privileges. An `adminId` document must exist here for a user to have admin rights, where `adminId` matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier for the administrator, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Records all administrative actions for auditing. Only accessible by administrators.

params.name: adminLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the admin log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, focusing on authorization independence, clarity of intent, and support for secure `list` operations (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **Team and Admin Roles:** For top-level collections like `/teams/{teamId}` and `/admin_roles/{adminId}`, authorization is directly tied to the document ID matching `request.auth.uid`. This makes them inherently authorization-independent as the UID itself is the authority, requiring no `get()` calls to other documents for fundamental access.
2.  **Hierarchical User-Owned Data:** The `/teams/{teamId}/attempts/{attemptId}` subcollection follows path-based ownership. The `teamId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making access rules simple (`request.auth.uid == teamId`) and atomic for operations within that subcollection. The `teamId` is implicitly denormalized into the path structure.
3.  **Collaborative/Shared Data (Hints):** The `/levels/{levelId}/hints/{hintId}` collection presents the most complex authorization challenge under strict adherence to Authorization Independence (avoiding `get()` calls). Hints need to be read only if `isReleased` is true AND the requesting team (`request.auth.uid`) is currently on the associated `levelId`. 
    *   **How Authorization Independence is handled:** To enable QAPs for hints (allowing clients to query `where isReleased == true` securely) and support real-time synchronization, the chosen approach allows a limited `get()` call within security rules: `get(/teams/$(request.auth.uid)).data.currentLevelId == levelId`. This reads the *authenticated user's own team document* to verify their current level. While a `get()` call, it is specifically for the `request.auth.uid`'s own state, which is a common and often accepted pattern, as it does not create a complex hierarchical dependency chain between arbitrary documents and does not break atomic operations involving the `hint` document itself. The `levelId` is included in the path to simplify rule evaluation, and `isReleased` is a direct field on the hint document.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**

1.  **Public vs. Sensitive Level Data:** The `/levels/{levelId}` collection stores public-facing level information (`question`, `order`). Sensitive data like `answerHash` and `salt` (as identified in the backend requirements) would reside within these same documents, but security rules would explicitly prevent clients from reading these specific fields. This maintains a single collection for levels while segregating read access at the field level, ensuring clients only receive public information.
2.  **Admin-Only Data:** Collections like `/flags/{flagId}` and `/admin_logs/{adminLogId}` are segregated. Their security posture is uniformly admin-only. Access is granted based on the existence of the `request.auth.uid` in the `/admin_roles/{adminId}` collection, ensuring strict administrative control and simplifying rules for these collections.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   **Team Data:** Teams can securely `list` their own attempts in `/teams/{teamId}/attempts`. The rules enforce that `teamId` matches `request.auth.uid`.
*   **Leaderboard:** The leaderboard functionality is implicitly supported. A Cloud Function could securely read and aggregate data from `teams` for the leaderboard, or security rules can allow public read of specific, non-sensitive `team` fields (`currentLevelId`, `teamName`) for the leaderboard, enabling client-side `list` operations.
*   **Hints:** For hints in `/levels/{levelId}/hints`, clients can `list` documents `where isReleased == true` for their `currentLevelId`. The security rule checks both the `isReleased` field (preventing rule filtering) and the team's current level (via the controlled `get()` for `request.auth.uid`'s own team document) to ensure only relevant and released hints are accessible.
*   **Admin Data:** Admins can securely `list` all documents in `/flags` and `/admin_logs` due to their explicit admin role definition in `/admin_roles/{adminId}`. This design enables efficient queries without requiring the rules to filter out unauthorized documents after a broad `list` operation.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims) and Data Clarity:**

*   **Global Roles:** Admin roles are managed via the `/admin_roles/{adminId}` collection, using the 'existence over content' principle. This decouples role management from Firebase Auth custom claims, simplifying rule logic (`get(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)) != null`).
*   **Predictable Schema & Consistent Naming:** All paths use descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{teamId}`, `{levelId}`). Authorization fields like `teamId` are consistently used either in the path or as a denormalized field where necessary, promoting clarity and debuggability in rules. Explicit state modeling for `isReleased` in hints simplifies conditional access.
